data.title: AbbVie and Calico win FDA fast track tag for kidney drug
data.description:
Calico Life Sciences announced on Thursday that the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) has issued Fast Track Designation for ABBV-CLS-628, a kidney disease therapy developed by the company in partnership with AbbVie (NYSE:ABBV).

The treatment targeting autosomal dominant polycystic kidney disease is currently undergoing a Phase 2 trial for the indication involving nearly 95 trial sites globally, the San Francisco, California-based biotech said.

Characterized by the formation of fluid-filled cysts in the kidneys, ADPKD is the most common genetically developed kidney disease in the world. More than 50% of patients with ADPKD can develop kidney failure before they reach 60 years, requiring dialysis or a transplant as the only available treatment options.

The FDA fast-track designation allows a faster timeline for the development and review of drugs for serious conditions with unmet medical needs. Subject to certain conditions, companies with fast-track designations can secure the FDA's accelerated approval and priority review to bring their products to market sooner.

data.title: FICO stock soars, credit rating bureau stocks drop after license model shift
data.description:
Fair Isaac (NYSE:FICO) stock surged 20% in Thursday morning trading after it announced on Wednesday a direct license program that gives resellers the option to calculate and distribute FICO stores directly to their customers.

That put pressure on the three major nationwide credit reporting companies — Equifax (EFX) -8.5%, TransUnion (TRU) -12%, and Experian (OTCQX:EXPGY) ADRs -4.8%.

"This shift will drive price transparency and immediate cost savings to mortgage lenders, mortgage brokers, and other industry participants," FICO said in a statement late Wednesday. "Firms that favor working through the credit bureaus can continue to do so."

FICO (NYSE:FICO) had been pressured by the Trump administration to reduce the fees it charges for its widely used FICO score. On July 8, FICO stock dropped 8.9% after the Federal Housing Finance Agency, which oversees mortgage giants Fannie Mae (OTCQB:FNMA) and Freddie Mac (OTCQB:FMCC), said it will allow the GSEs to use VantageScore 4.0 for assessing borrowers' creditworthiness. VantageScore is a joint venture of Equifax, TransUnion, and Experian.

The FICO score is used by almost 90% of lenders to evaluate a borrower's creditworthiness.

Under the new model, the royalty fee for the FICO score will be $4.95 per score, a 50% reduction in average per-score fees into the tri-merge resellers.  

A funded loan fee of $33 per borrower per score will apply when a FICO-scored loan is closed, recognizing the FICO Score’s downstream utility for mortgage insurers, GSEs, investors, rating agencies, and other market participants. The funded loan fee replaces fees previously charged for re-issue of FICO Scores, enabling broad use by participants in the originating market.

The news "implies that this would cut out the margin that the likes of Experian and Equifax make on the FICO credit score itself," said Citi analyst Arthur Truslove in a note to clients. "We would expect more information to come out through the day, but our initial reaction is that this is negative for Experian and Equifax."

data.title: Markwayne Mullin discloses purchases of Texas Instruments, ServiceNow, Adobe, more
data.description:
• Sen. Markwayne Mullin (R-OK) has disclosed that he purchased positions in several companies, including stakes in technology companies such as Texas Instruments (NASDAQ:TXN), ServiceNow (NYSE:NOW) and Adobe (NASDAQ:ADBE). 
  • A new congressional trading form filed by Mullin disclosed that he purchased the positions on Sept. 24, with one stake valued between $1,001 and $15,000. Other stakes were valued between $15,001 and $50,000 and another stake was valued at up to $100,000.
  • Mullin's purchases of Adobe, UnitedHealth Group (UNH), Intuit (INTU), Accenture (ACN), Texas Instruments (NASDAQ:TXN) and iShares U.S. Health Care Providers ETF (IHF) were valued between $15,001 and $50,000. His purchase of ServiceNow was valued between $1,001 and $15,000, while his purchase of Intuitive Surgical (ISRG) was valued between $50,001 and $100,000.
  • Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Healthcare’s bounce has some profit strength issues – Société Générale
data.description:
Healthcare stocks (IYH), (NYSEARCA:XLV), (VHT) could be headed towards a rebound, but the strength of its profit cycle is not convincing, according to Société Générale.

Manish Kabra, head of U.S. Equity Strategy and Multi-Asset Strategy at Société Générale, said healthcare’s (NYSEARCA:XLV) extreme underperformance can “trigger a short-term bounce” as any sector that has lagged substantially “is worth taking a closer look.”

But “unlocking sustained value requites stronger relative EPS outperformance, which is not evident,” he added.

Kabra recommended investors to trade the healthcare sector “opportunistically” as the market looks for “catch-up trades” for this year. He sees the S&P 500 (SP500) gaining through 2026’s first quarter to 7,300 points at 22x multiple on $333 2027 EPS.

However, to give an “overweight” rating on healthcare (XLV), “we need more convincing evidence of relative profit cycle strength, which has so far failed over the past five years,” he said.

Both the relative performance of healthcare to the S&P 500 (SP500) on a rolling six-month basis and 12-month basis is on the lowest levels since at least 2016 and 2006, respectively.

data.title: Eni OKs $7.2B Coral North floating LNG export project offshore Mozambique
data.description:
Eni (NYSE:E) said Thursday it reached a final investment decision to proceed with Mozambique's second floating liquefied natural gas platform, which is expected to produce 3.6 million tons/year of LNG.

The new Coral North unit, a replica of the Coral South platform in the Rovuma Basin, will double Mozambique's LNG production to more than 7 million tons/year when it becomes operational.

The project will be implemented by a joint venture between operator and 50%-owner Eni (NYSE:E), China National Petroleum Company, Korea Gas, Mozambique state-owned national oil company ENH, and Adnoc subsidiary XRG.

Separately, Eni (E) said earlier this week that it acquired the CI-707 offshore block offshore exploration block in Côte d'Ivoire.

The acquisition adds to its presence in Côte d'Ivoire, where it produces more than 62,000 bbl/day of oil and more than 75 million cf/day of gas, which is set to rise to 150,000 bbl/day of oil and 200 million cf/day of gas.

data.title: Nanobiotix gains after early-stage trial data for J&J-partnered cancer drug
data.description:
Nanobiotix (NASDAQ:NBTX) traded higher on Thursday after the French biotech announced initial results from a Phase 1 trial for JNJ-1900 (NBTXR3), a cancer injection developed by the company in partnership with Johnson & Johnson (NYSE:JNJ) for solid tumors.

The results represented the first and second cohorts in a dose escalation portion of a Phase 1 study designed to evaluate the intratumoral injection as part of a combination therapy for a type of esophageal cancer.

Citing a presentation conducted at a medical event on Wednesday, Paris-based Nanobiotix (NASDAQ:NBTX) said that JNJ-1900, when added to photon chemoradiation or proton chemoradiation, caused an 85% disease control rate.

Other findings indicated a 69% objective response rate, including six complete remissions and three partial remissions, as well as a well-tolerated safety profile for the experimental treatment, according to the company.

The readout was based on data from 13 patients with locally advanced adenocarcinoma of the esophagus who, if medically indicated, underwent surgery following the drug combo.

Enrollment in Cohort 1 is complete, Nanobiotix (NASDAQ:NBTX) said, adding that it is recruiting five more patients for Cohort 2 and seeks to add another 12 patients for the dose expansion section of the study.

data.title: Disparity is on the menu: Restaurant stocks deliver a wide mix of returns
data.description:
Unlike many industries where stocks tend to move in the same general direction, the restaurant sector has proven far more fragmented in 2025. Performance among food and beverage names has varied widely, with some companies delivering outsized gains while others have suffered steep declines.

For example, Potbelly (PBPB) has surged more than 80% year-to-date, standing out as one of the strongest performers in the space. By contrast, Sweetgreen (SG) has collapsed by nearly 75%, underscoring the uneven landscape investors face when navigating this group. 

This divergence highlights the importance of stock-specific fundamentals, as broader industry momentum has offered little predictive value. 

Below is a breakdown of 32 restaurant stocks that illustrates the striking disparity in performance across the sector. 

No. 1: Potbelly (PBPB) +81% YTD.

No. 2: Nathan’s Famous (NATH) +41.2% YTD. 

No. 3: Biglari (BH) +27% YTD.

data.title: U.S. looks to ease UK whisky imports, lowering risk of retaliatory tariffs on U.S. counterparts - report
data.description:
Washington is weighing tariff relief on imported UK whisky, a step that would remove the threat of retaliatory tariffs on U.S. bourbon while at the same time benefit American distillers that supply used oak barrels to UK producers.

According to Bloomberg, U.S. officials have discussed the possibility to carve out tariff exemptions for UK whisky producers, currently subject to a 10% tax to import their whisky into the U.S.

According to data out of the Scotch Whisky Association, the trade barrier costs UK distillers £20M per month ($26.9M) in lost exports, while 60% of all UK scotch is aged in barrels from Kentucky.

Although the U.S. and UK reached a trade agreement in May to exempt certain UK industries, the UK whisky business is still subject to a 10% tax, leaving domestic producers like Brown-Foreman (BF.A) (NYSE:BF.B), Diageo (DEO) -- which makes rum in the U.S. Virgin Islands -- and MGP Ingredients (MGPI) vulnerable to retaliatory tariffs.

Additionally, because American law requires all bourbon to be aged in new oak barrels, a majority of the used barrels are sold to foreign counterparts to be used again (some barrels are reused to age scotch, rum, tequila, and craft beer), opening up another revenue source for U.S. industries.

data.title: Yahoo in talks to sell AOL as owner Apollo eyes $1.4B deal with Italian tech firm
data.description:
Apollo Global-owned company Yahoo (NYSE:APO) is in advanced talks to sell AOL to Italy's Bending Spoons for about $1.4B, Reuters reported late on Wednesday, citing four sources.

The Milan-based technology company is in advanced talks to purchase the legacy media brand from the U.S.-based asset manager, but sources told the news agency that a final agreement has not been signed and talks could still fall apart.

The deal would add AOL's vast user base to Bending Spoons' portfolio of mobile applications, the report said.

AOL, which makes money from advertising and through its subscription services, including LifeLock identity theft protection, LastPass password management, and McAfee Multi Access malware protection, recently saw its website traffic grow 20% from last year among the users aged 25 and 54, outpacing the growth in the category of users aged 55-plus, a source familiar with AOL's performance told Reuters.

The growth was driven by the introduction of multiple new content categories to AOL.com, including Health, Fitness, Animals, Science & Tech, Home & Garden, Lighter Side, True Crime, and Local, amongst others, the source told Reuters.

Bending Spoons purchases struggling tech firms and revamps them. The company has 300M monthly users across its products and has done several acquisitions recently. Last month it agreed to a deal to take private video platform company Vimeo for $1.38B, which is its largest acquisition to date.

data.title: SoFi starts offering no-fee options trading for beginners
data.description:
SoFi Technologies (NASDAQ:SOFI) started rolling out no-fee options products for retail investors who are new to the market, the company said Thursday. It will be available to all eligible SoFi Invest members in coming weeks.

Members who are approved will gain access for so-called level 1 trading, including covered calls and cash-secured puts, in addition to the options level 2 strategies already available. The aim is to help investors better manage their portfolio, generate premium income, enter new positions, and hedge existing ones to manage risk, SoFi (NASDAQ:SOFI) said.

"One of the most consistent requests we heard from SoFi Invest members was access to Options Level 1, and we’re thrilled to deliver on that demand," said Anthony Noto, CEO of SoFi (NASDAQ:SOFI). "By expanding access to options trading and embedding education directly into the experience, members can responsibly diversify their strategies and pursue their long-term financial goals with confidence, all within SoFi’s one-stop shop."

The company is removing fees associated with options exercise and assignment in an effort to make options trading smoother and more transparent and to reduce costs for active options traders, it said.

Eligibility is determined by factors such as trading experience, investment objectives, and financial situation.

SoFi (NASDAQ:SOFI) also introduced alternative funds from ARK, KKR (KKR), Carlyle Group (CG), and Franklin-Templeton (BEN), covering private credit, real estate, and pre-IPO firm. In addition, it relaunched its robo-advisor in partnership with BlackRock (BLK), which also includes portfolios that invest in alternative assets.

Note that the company warned that "options trading involves significant risk and is not suitable for all investors."

SoFi (SOFI) stock slipped 0.4% in Thursday morning trading.
